title: Introduction to fixes
text: fixes package provides easy--use toolkit creating, estimating, visualizing event study models using fixed effects regression. fixes, can automatically generate lead lag dummy variables, flexibly estimate fixed effects event study regressions, visualize results ggplot2 using single pipeline. vignette introduces core functions package simple examples, including recent updates multiple confidence interval support improved plotting options.

title: Introduction to fixes
text: Install released version CRAN: pak (recommended fast install): install latest development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("fixes") pak::pak("fixes") pak::pak("yo5uke/fixes") devtools::install_github("yo5uke/fixes")

title: Introduction to fixes
text: basic example using built-fixest::base_did dataset, running event study, visualizing results.
code: # Load example data df <- fixest::base_did  # Run the event study (supports multiple confidence levels) event_study <- run_es(   data       = df,   outcome    = y,   treatment  = treat,   time       = period,   timing     = 5,  # Treatment occurs at period 5   fe         = ~ id + period,   cluster    = ~ id,   baseline   = -1,   interval   = 1,   lead_range = 3,   lag_range  = 3,   conf.level = c(0.90, 0.95, 0.99)  # Multiple CIs supported! )  # View results head(event_study) #> Event Study Result (fixes) #>   N: 1080  | Units: NA  | Treated units: 1080  | Never-treated: NA  #>   FE: id + period #>   VCOV: cluster  | Cluster: id  #>   Method: classic  | lead_range: 3  lag_range: 3  baseline: -1

title: Introduction to fixes
text: fixes package provides plot_es() flexible visualization. can easily switch ribbon-style error bar CIs, select displayed CI level, customize appearance.
code: # Basic plot (default: ribbon, 95% CI) plot_es(event_study) # Plot with error bars and 99% CI plot_es(event_study, type = "errorbar", ci_level = 0.99) # Customize further with ggplot2 plot_es(event_study, type = "errorbar", ci_level = 0.9, theme_style = "classic") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-3, 3, by = 1)) +   ggtitle("Event Study with 90% CI and Classic Theme")

title: Introduction to fixes
text: staggered adoption designs treatment timing varies across units, can use method = "sunab" implement Sun & Abraham (2021) estimator, robust heterogeneous treatment effects.  New v0.7.1: baseline parameter now applies classic sunab methods, baseline period included results zero estimates consistent visualization.
code: # Example with fixest::base_stagg data df_stagg <- fixest::base_stagg  event_study_sunab <- run_es(   data       = df_stagg,   outcome    = y,   treatment  = treated,   time       = year,   timing     = year_treated,   fe         = ~ id + year,   staggered  = TRUE,   method     = "sunab",  # Use Sun & Abraham decomposition   lead_range = 3,   lag_range  = 3,   cluster    = ~ id )  head(event_study_sunab) #> Event Study Result (fixes) #>   N: 950  | Units: NA  | Treated units: 950  | Never-treated: NA  #>   FE: id + year #>   VCOV: cluster  | Cluster: id  #>   Method: SUNAB (staggered-safe) # Visualize sunab results plot_es(event_study_sunab) +   ggtitle("Staggered Adoption Event Study (Sun & Abraham 2021)")

title: Introduction to fixes
text: Fast, one-step event study panel data. Automatic creation lead/lag dummies relative treatment. Supports classic staggered timing, covariates, clustering, weights, flexible baseline normalization. Choice estimation methods: classic (factor expansion) sunab (Sun & Abraham 2021 staggered designs). Multiple confidence interval levels supported (e.g., 90%, 95%, 99%). Handles irregular time panels via time_transform. Results filtered specified lead_range lag_range (v0.7.1+). Intuitive event study plot ribbon errorbar CI display. CI level visual style fully customizable. ggplot2-based modification. Interactive plotly-based visualizations hover tooltips. Displays point estimates, confidence intervals, standard errors, p-values hover.

title: Introduction to fixes
text: Standard TWFE event-study regressions can produce biased sign-reversed estimates heterogeneous treatment effects units adopt treatment different times (Callaway & Sant’Anna 2021; Sun & Abraham 2021; Borusyak, Jaravel & Spiess 2024). fixes v0.8.0 adds three robust alternatives, accessible run_es() interface via estimator argument. demonstrate three fixest::base_stagg, simulated staggered-adoption panel true ATT = 1 cohorts horizons.
code: df_stagg <- fixest::base_stagg # Mark never-treated units with NA (convention for all three estimators) df_stagg$timing <- df_stagg$year_treated df_stagg$timing[df_stagg$year_treated == 10000] <- NA

title: Introduction to fixes
text: Estimates separate ATT(g,t) every cohort--period cell, aggregates event-study curve using cohort-size weights.
code: res_cs <- run_es(   data          = df_stagg,   outcome       = y,   time          = year,   timing        = timing,   unit          = id,   staggered     = TRUE,   estimator     = "cs",   control_group = "nevertreated" ) plot_es(res_cs) + ggplot2::ggtitle("Callaway & Sant'Anna (2021)")

title: Introduction to fixes
text: Builds cohort x relative-time interactions aggregates cohort-share weights. Numerically identical fixest::sunab().
code: res_sa <- run_es(   data      = df_stagg,   outcome   = y,   treatment = treated,   time      = year,   timing    = timing,   unit      = id,   fe        = ~ id + year,   staggered = TRUE,   estimator = "sa",   cluster   = ~ id ) plot_es(res_sa) + ggplot2::ggtitle("Sun & Abraham (2021)")

title: Introduction to fixes
text: Fits TWFE untreated observations, imputes counterfactuals treated units, averages horizon.  homogeneous treatment effects (DGP), three estimators give similar results, recovering post-treatment ATT close true value 1.
code: res_bjs <- run_es(   data      = df_stagg,   outcome   = y,   time      = year,   timing    = timing,   unit      = id,   staggered = TRUE,   estimator = "bjs" ) plot_es(res_bjs) + ggplot2::ggtitle("Borusyak, Jaravel & Spiess (2024)")

title: Introduction to fixes
text: Standard confidence intervals pointwise: 95% CI horizon covers true value 95% probability, probability intervals simultaneously cover true values can much lower many periods plotted. Simultaneous confidence bands (Callaway & Sant’Anna 2021, Corollary 1) control joint coverage probability. probability least 1 − α, entire event-study curve contained within simultaneous band. especially important parallel-trends pre-testing: pre-trend test based simultaneous bands controls family-wise error rate.

title: Introduction to fixes
text: Pass bootstrap = TRUE run_es() together CS estimator. multiplier bootstrap (Algorithm 1 Callaway & Sant’Anna 2021) used. simultaneous critical value ĉ per-period simultaneous CI bounds stored attr(res_cs_boot, "bootstrap"). simultaneous band always least wide pointwise band, since critical value ĉ_{1-α} >= z_{1-α/2}.
code: # NOTE: B = 199 shown here for brevity; use B = 999 in practice res_cs_boot <- run_es(   data          = df_stagg,   outcome       = y,   time          = year,   timing        = timing,   unit          = id,   staggered     = TRUE,   estimator     = "cs",   control_group = "nevertreated",   bootstrap     = TRUE,   B             = 199,   boot_seed     = 42 ) # The lighter outer band is the simultaneous CI; the darker inner band is # the standard pointwise CI. plot_es(res_cs_boot, show_simultaneous = TRUE)

title: Staggered DiD Tools: Event Studies and ATT Aggregation
text: simple two-way FE single treatment coefficient, use run_did(). Output fully compatible modelsummary::modelsummary() tinytable::tt(). two equivalent ways specify treatment: options produce did_result object: run_did() integrates broom modelsummary ecosystems:
code: # Option A: supply a pre-built D_it indicator df$D <- as.integer(df$treated & df$year >= 2006) res <- run_did(df, outcome = y, treatment = D, fe = ~ id + year) # Option B: let run_did() construct D_it from group indicator + timing res <- run_did(df, outcome = y, treatment = treated,                time = year, timing = 2006,                fe = ~ id + year) df <- fixest::base_did  # Build a universal-timing DiD dataset df$D <- as.integer(df$treat == 1 & df$period >= 5)  res <- run_did(   data    = df,   outcome = y,   treatment = D,   fe      = ~ id + period,   cluster = ~ id )  print(res) ## DiD Estimation  [TWFE] ## N = 1080 obs | 330 treated obs ## FE: id + period  ## VCOV: cluster | Cluster: id  ##  ##   term estimate std.error statistic  p.value ## 1    D      4.5     0.544      8.27 3.94e-13 broom::tidy(res)          # all coefficients (treatment + any covariates) broom::glance(res)        # nobs, within R², AIC, ... modelsummary::modelsummary(res)   # regression table via tinytable

title: Staggered DiD Tools: Event Studies and ATT Aggregation
text: Event-study estimates rely parallel trends assumption. Rather testing pre-trends hoping best, honest_sensitivity() implements Rambachan & Roth (2023): reports confidence sets post-treatment effect progressively weaker restrictions different post-treatment violations parallel trends can pre-trends, plus breakdown value — largest violation effect still significant. Use type = "smoothness" (bounded second difference) restriction. Inference uses Andrews-Roth-Pakes conditional test — pure-R reimplementation validated HonestDiD package. estimators "twfe", pass betahat sigma directly. numeric helpers (lpSolveAPI, Rglpk, TruncatedNormal, Matrix, pracma) optional Suggests.
code: res <- run_es(df, outcome = y, treatment = treat, time = year, timing = 6,               fe = ~ id + year)  # Relative-magnitude restriction: post violation <= Mbar x max pre violation h <- honest_sensitivity(res, type = "relative_magnitude",                         Mvec = c(0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2)) print(h)          # robust CIs per Mbar + the original (parallel-trends) CI plot_honest(h)    # "top-down" sensitivity plot

what: Compute ATT Aggregations for Staggered Adoption Designs — calc_att
title: Compute ATT Aggregations for Staggered Adoption Designs — calc_att
text: Estimates average treatment effects treated (ATT) using either Callaway-Sant'Anna (2021) Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess (2024) estimator, aggregated single summary effect ("simple"), per-cohort effects ("by_cohort"), per calendar-time effects ("by_time").

title: Compute ATT Aggregations for Staggered Adoption Designs — calc_att
code: calc_att(   data,   outcome,   treatment = NULL,   time,   timing,   fe = NULL,   covariates = NULL,   cluster = NULL,   weights = NULL,   interval = 1,   time_transform = FALSE,   unit = NULL,   estimator = c("cs", "bjs"),   aggregation = c("simple", "by_cohort", "by_time"),   control_group = c("nevertreated", "notyettreated"),   anticipation = 0L,   conf.level = 0.95,   vcov = "HC1",   vcov_args = list() )

title: Compute ATT Aggregations for Staggered Adoption Designs — calc_att
text: data data.frame containing panel data. outcome Unquoted outcome variable (name expression, e.g., log(y)). treatment Unused; reserved future use. time Unquoted calendar time variable (numeric). timing Unquoted column giving unit's first treatment period (NA = never treated). fe Ignored (CS BJS absorb fixed effects internally). covariates Ignored; reserved future use. cluster Ignored; reserved future use. weights Ignored; reserved future use. interval Numeric time spacing (default 1; informational ). time_transform Logical; TRUE, creates consecutive integer time within unit via dplyr::dense_rank(). Requires unit. unit Unquoted unit identifier (required). estimator Estimation strategy: "cs" (Callaway-Sant'Anna 2021, default) "bjs" (Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess 2024). aggregation Aggregation type: "simple" (overall ATT, default), "by_cohort" (one ATT per treatment cohort), "by_time" (one ATT per calendar time period). control_group estimator = "cs": comparison group, "nevertreated" (default) "notyettreated". anticipation estimator = "cs": number anticipation periods treatment (non-negative integer, default 0L). conf.level Numeric confidence level(s) (default 0.95). Multiple levels supported, e.g., c(0.90, 0.95). vcov Ignored (SE analytical CS; approximate BJS). vcov_args Ignored.

title: Compute ATT Aggregations for Staggered Adoption Designs — calc_att
text: data.frame class "att_result" columns: group Cohort calendar time (NA "simple"). estimate ATT point estimate. std.error Standard error. statistic t-statistic (estimate / std.error). p.value Two-sided p-value (normal approximation). conf_low_XX, conf_high_XX CI bounds conf.level. Attributes: aggregation, estimator, conf.level, N, N_units, N_treated, N_nevertreated, control_group (CS ), att_gt (CS raw ATT(g,t) table), tau_it (BJS unit-time effects table).

title: Compute ATT Aggregations for Staggered Adoption Designs — calc_att
text: function complements run_es(): use run_es() want full event-study curve (dynamic effects relative time), calc_att() want aggregated ATT estimates collapse time dimension.

title: Compute ATT Aggregations for Staggered Adoption Designs — calc_att
text: simple: \(\theta = \sum_g (n_g/n_{treated}) \cdot \overline{ATT(g,\cdot)}\) \(\overline{ATT(g,\cdot)}\) mean post-treatment periods. by_cohort: \(\theta(g) = \overline{ATT(g,\cdot)}\) per cohort. by_time: \(\theta(t) = \sum_{g \le t} w(g,t) \cdot ATT(g,t)\) \(w(g,t) = n_g / \sum_{g' \le t} n_{g'}\).

title: Compute ATT Aggregations for Staggered Adoption Designs — calc_att
text: BJS SEs approximate (naive sample variance unit-time effects). Cluster-robust SE BJS aggregations planned v0.12.

what: Contamination Weights for TWFE Event-Study Coefficients (Sun-Abraham 2021) — compute_contamination_weights
title: Contamination Weights for TWFE Event-Study Coefficients (Sun-Abraham 2021) — compute_contamination_weights
text: Estimates contamination weights \(\omega^{\ell''}_{e,\ell}\) decompose TWFE event-study coefficient \(\hat\mu_{\ell''}\) linear combination cohort-specific ATTs (CATTs): $$\hat\mu_{\ell''} = \sum_{(e,\ell)} \omega^{\ell''}_{e,\ell} \cdot   \widehat{CATT}_{e,\ell}$$ (Sun Abraham 2021, Equation 20). weights obtained via OVB auxiliary regression (Eq. 12): cohort-period CATT cell \((e, \ell)\), regress cohort-specific indicator \(1\{E_i = e\}\cdot 1\{t-E_i = \ell\}\) cohort-aggregated relative-time indicators \(D^{\ell''}_{,t}\) two-way fixed effects. resulting regression coefficient \(D^{\ell''}_{,t}\) \(\omega^{\ell''}_{e,\ell}\).

title: Contamination Weights for TWFE Event-Study Coefficients (Sun-Abraham 2021) — compute_contamination_weights
code: compute_contamination_weights(   data,   time,   timing,   unit,   fe = NULL,   baseline = -1L )

title: Contamination Weights for TWFE Event-Study Coefficients (Sun-Abraham 2021) — compute_contamination_weights
text: data data.frame one row per unit-period (balanced panel). time Unquoted name calendar time variable (numeric). timing Unquoted name first-treatment-period variable; NA marks never-treated units. unit Unquoted name unit identifier. fe One-sided fixed-effects formula, e.g. ~ id + year. NULL (default), falls back ~ <unit> + <time> warning. baseline Integer reference (baseline) period excluded TWFE specification (default -1L).  Must match baseline argument used run_es().

title: Contamination Weights for TWFE Event-Study Coefficients (Sun-Abraham 2021) — compute_contamination_weights
text: object class c("sa_contamination_weights", "data.frame") one row per (catt_cohort, catt_period, twfe_period) triple, columns: catt_cohort Cohort \(e\) (first treatment period). catt_period Relative event time \(\ell\) CATT. twfe_period Relative event time \(\ell''\) TWFE coefficient decomposed. weight Contamination weight \(\omega^{\ell''}_{e,\ell}\). is_own Logical; TRUE catt_period == twfe_period. Attributes: baseline, cohorts, cohort_sizes, incl_periods.

title: Contamination Weights for TWFE Event-Study Coefficients (Sun-Abraham 2021) — compute_contamination_weights
text: -period cell (catt_period == twfe_period): \(\omega^{\ell}_{{e,\ell}}\) represents weight TWFE estimator places \(CATT_{e,\ell}\). SA IW estimator equal cohort-size weights \(n_e / \sum n_{e'}\). Cross-period cell (catt_period != twfe_period): non-zero weight indicates contamination: TWFE coefficient \(\hat\mu_{\ell''}\) also picks treatment effects period \(\ell \ne \ell''\). Verification: OVB identity (property iii) holds exactly, \(\hat\mu_{\ell''} = \sum_{(e,\ell)}   \omega^{\ell''}_{e,\ell} \cdot \widehat{CATT}_{e,\ell}\) floating-point precision.

title: Contamination Weights for TWFE Event-Study Coefficients (Sun-Abraham 2021) — compute_contamination_weights
text: Sun, L. Abraham, S. (2021). Estimating dynamic treatment effects event studies heterogeneous treatment effects. Journal Econometrics, 225(2), 175–199.

what: Honest sensitivity analysis for parallel-trends violations — honest_sensitivity
title: Honest sensitivity analysis for parallel-trends violations — honest_sensitivity
text: Robust inference sensitivity analysis event-study / designs following Rambachan Roth (2023).  Instead assuming parallel trends holds exactly, asks large violation parallel trends causal conclusion changes, returning confidence sets post-treatment effect sequence restrictions possible difference trends. Two restriction families supported: "relative_magnitude" (\(\Delta^{RM}(\bar M)\)): post-treatment violations \(\bar M\) times largest pre-treatment violation (Rambachan & Roth 2023, Section 2.4.1). "smoothness" (\(\Delta^{SD}(M)\)): difference trends deviates linearity \(M\) per period (Section 2.4.3). Inference uses Andrews-Roth-Pakes (ARP) conditional moment-inequality test (Section 3.2.1), uniformly valid recommended general restriction sets.

what: Plot the ATT(g,t) matrix from a Callaway-Sant'Anna event study — plot_att_gt
title: Plot the ATT(g,t) matrix from a Callaway-Sant'Anna event study — plot_att_gt
text: Visualises full cohort--period ATT(g,t) matrix stored att_gt attribute es_result object produced run_es(estimator = "cs").  Two display styles available: "heatmap": tile plot calendar time \(t\) x-axis cohort \(g\) y-axis, colour-filled point estimate.  Cells whose pointwise confidence interval excludes zero marked filled dot; cells simultaneously significant (bootstrap data available) additionally marked open diamond. "facet": one panel per cohort showing ATT(g,t) calendar time \(t\) pointwise confidence ribbon, mirroring style plot_es().  lighter simultaneous CI ribbon overlaid bootstrap data available. types draw vertical dashed line \(t = g\) (treatment onset) cohort.

what: Run a basic two-way fixed-effects DiD model — run_did
title: Run a basic two-way fixed-effects DiD model — run_did
text: Estimates classic difference--differences model form outcome ~ D_it | fe using fixest::feols(). two ways supply treatment indicator: Option — pre-built D_it (maximum flexibility):   Option B — timing-based construction (convenience; consistent run_es() calc_att()):   treatment binary group indicator (1 = treated unit, 0 = control), time calendar-time variable, timing scalar treatment onset period.  Internally D_it = treatment * (time >= timing) constructed automatically.  staggered-adoption settings use calc_att(); dynamic event-study estimates use run_es().
code: df$D <- as.integer(df$treated & df$year >= 2006) run_did(df, outcome = y, treatment = D, fe = ~ id + year) run_did(df, outcome = y, treatment = treated, time = year, timing = 2006,         fe = ~ id + year)

Default "HC1". vcov_args List additional arguments forwarded fixest::vcov(). bootstrap Logical; TRUE estimator = "cs", compute simultaneous confidence bands via multiplier bootstrap (Algorithm 1, Callaway Sant'Anna 2021).  Adds conf_low_sim conf_high_sim columns result stores full (g,t)-level bootstrap object attr(result, "bootstrap").  Default FALSE. B Integer number bootstrap draws (default 999).  Used bootstrap = TRUE estimator = "cs". alpha Significance level simultaneous band (default 0.05). Note: independent conf.level, governs pointwise delta-method CIs. boot_seed Integer seed bootstrap RNG; NULL (default) set seed.  Pass integer reproducible results. group Unquoted group identifier estimator = "flex" . Identifies treatment group (cohort) observation belongs repeated cross-section design (\(R_{ig}\) Deb et al. 2024).  group must map exactly one value timing (NA never-treated groups).  used estimators. trends Logical; estimator = "twm" .  TRUE, adds cohort-specific linear trend regressors \(d_g \cdot t\) Procedure 5.1 regression (Wooldridge 2025, Section 8), allowing cohort's counterfactual trend deviate linearly common time trend. text: honest_sensitivity() — honest robust inference (Rambachan & Roth 2023) (R/honest_did.R): sensitivity analysis event-study / designs parallel trends may violated. Instead assuming parallel trends holds exactly, reports confidence sets post-treatment effect progressively weaker restrictions difference trends, breakdown value (largest restriction effect still significant): Restriction families: "relative_magnitude" () "smoothness" (). title: fixes 0.11.0 (2026-05-24)
text: Estimator-core consolidation (behaviour-preserving): factored boilerplate duplicated across run_es(), calc_att(), run_did(), five estimator backends shared helpers R/utils-internal.R — .resolve_col() (NSE column resolution), .add_ci_columns() (CI construction), .validate_panel_cols(), .compute_cohort_sizes(), .model_vcov_full(), .aggregate_iw(). calc_att() aggregation loops vectorised. existing tests pass unchanged. run_did() — basic TWFE estimator (R/run_did.R): New function classic two-way fixed-effects difference--differences. Accepts pre-built binary treatment indicator D_it returns did_result S3 object full modelsummary / tinytable compatibility: NSE API consistent run_es() calc_att() — specify variables name, formula construction. fe auto-inferred ~ unit + time unit time supplied fe = NULL. tidy.did_result() delegates broom::tidy.fixest() full regression table (regressors); glance.did_result() delegates broom::glance.fixest() model-level stats (within.r.squared, nobs, AIC, etc.). print.did_result() shows clean header: estimator, sample sizes, FE spec, VCOV type. Outcome expressions like log(y) supported. modelsummary tinytable added Suggests. calc_att() — ATT aggregation function (R/calc_att.R): New function separates “calculating ATT” “running event study”. run_es() produces full dynamic event-study curve (effects relative time), calc_att() yields aggregated ATT estimates clear, minimal interface: Estimators: "cs" (Callaway-Sant’Anna 2021) "bjs" (Borusyak et al. 2024). SA, TWM, FLEX event-study-estimators supported (use run_es()). "simple": exposure-weighted average post-treatment (g,t) pairs. "by_cohort": mean ATT(g,t) post-treatment periods per cohort. "by_time": cohort-size-weighted ATT per calendar period. SEs: independence assumption (diagonal delta-method). Systematically smaller ::aggte SEs, use influence-function variance accounting within-cohort time correlation. Cluster-robust SE BJS aggregations planned v0.12. Returns att_result S3 object (data.frame subclass) columns group, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value, conf_low_XX/conf_high_XX. Attributes: aggregation, estimator, N, N_units, N_treated, control_group (CS ), att_gt (raw CS ATT(g,t) table), tau_it (BJS unit-time effects table). print.att_result() S3 method provides concise summary.

title: fixes 0.8.1 (2026-05-10)
text: run_es(estimator = "cs"): Callaway-Sant’Anna (2021) estimator. Computes group-time ATT(g,t) via unconditional estimand (eq. 2.8) never-treated -yet-treated control groups. Supports control_group = "nevertreated" (default) "notyettreated". full ATT(g,t) matrix stored att_gt attribute result. run_es(estimator = "sa"): Sun-Abraham (2021) interaction-weighted estimator. Aggregates cohort x relative-time interactions cohort share weights. Numerically identical fixest::sunab() machine precision. run_es(estimator = "bjs"): Borusyak, Jaravel & Spiess (2024) imputation estimator. Fits TWFE untreated observations , imputes counterfactuals treated observations, averages treatment effects horizon. Handles singleton unit fixed effects via closed-form recovery. run_es(bootstrap = TRUE): Multiplier bootstrap simultaneous confidence bands (Algorithm 1, Callaway & Sant’Anna 2021). Adds conf_low_sim / conf_high_sim columns result stores (g,t)-level bootstrap object attr(result, "bootstrap"). Controlled B (draws, default 999) boot_seed arguments. Available estimator = "cs". plot_att_gt(): Visualize full ATT(g,t) matrix CS results heatmap (type = "heatmap") cohort-faceted time series (type = "facet"). Requires estimator = "cs" result input. bootstrap = TRUE used, heatmap adds subtitle simultaneous critical value open-diamond markers simultaneously significant cells; facet adds lighter simultaneous CI ribbon. plot_es(show_simultaneous = TRUE): Overlays simultaneous bootstrap CI (lighter band, alpha 0.15) alongside pointwise CI (alpha 0.3) two-entry legend. Errors informatively bootstrap run. plot_es_interactive(show_simultaneous = TRUE): Adds second lighter ribbon trace simultaneous CI extends hover tooltip simultaneous CI bounds.
